disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Repeated focal (right-sided) seizures over ~90 minutes, three to four in a row
trigger_question_examples: How many seizures has he had and over what time?
How long has this been going on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's had three or four seizures down his right side over about the last hour and a half, one after another.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Failure to regain consciousness between seizures — remains deeply drowsy and unresponsive
trigger_question_examples: Does he wake up or come round between the seizures?
Is he responding to you at all right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — he won't wake up at all between them, he's just completely floppy and not responding.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Home rescue benzodiazepine (buccal midazolam) already given, with failed efficacy
trigger_question_examples: Have you given his rescue medicine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, we gave the buccal midazolam about 45 minutes ago after the second seizure, but he had another one anyway.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A further seizure is occurring now — rhythmic twitching of the right face and right arm
trigger_question_examples: Is he having a seizure right now?
What is he doing at this moment?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's twitching on the right side of his face and his right arm right now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Weakness on the right side today is worse than the child's usual baseline
trigger_question_examples: Is his weakness worse than his usual right-sided limp?
Can he move his right arm and leg at all compared to normal?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Normally he has a bit of a weak right side but can still move it — right now that whole side is completely limp, much worse than usual.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known congenital condition (Sturge-Weber syndrome) with a longstanding difficult-to-control seizure disorder and baseline right-sided weakness
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any medical conditions?
Tell me about his usual health.
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He was born with Sturge-Weber syndrome — he has a birthmark on his face, hard-to-control seizures, weakness on his right side and glaucoma in his left eye.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No high fever and no neck stiffness reported
trigger_question_examples: Does his neck seem stiff or does he cry when you bend it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He doesn't feel especially hot and his neck seems okay — he's just not waking up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Breathing appears shallow and the child is very drowsy
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His breathing looks shallow and quick, and he's just so drowsy he won't rouse.
